creator: mikkel@troest.dk
text:
Hi Martin,

The patch file is there.
I'll go ahead and build OOO again with --enable-symbols=TRUE.
Regarding the root/non-root issue, I discovered that it was actually 
also environment-related since i did nont pass the OOO-force-desktop env 
to the root context when I sudoed. Thus it was NOT a permissions-issue.

I'll upload new dumps/traces later today (if my build-server behaves :)

:O) Mikkel

Marcin Cieslak wrote:
> Mikkel Troest wrote:
>> Hi,
>>
> >
> > Marcin Cieslak wrote:
> >>
> >> Can you try the patch that has recently been commited?
> >>
> >> editors/openoffice.org-2/files/patch-i75190
>
>> Sorry, but I don't know how to go about that, unless it's being 
>> applied automatically when building from the ports tree...
>> - And in that case, my latest test-build already includes this patch...
>
> Can you check if in your /usr/ports/editors/openoffice.org-2/files 
> there is a "patch-i75190".
>
>
>> Do you need a fresh trace, or do I need to do anything specific to 
>> get the patch included when building?
>
> If you are going to rebuild OOO, could you just change 
> "--enable-symbols=SMALL" to "--enable-symbols=TRUE" in 
> files/Makefile.knobs and then, when the ooo crashes preserve the 
> "soffice.bin.core" file
>
> and run
>
> gdb /usr/local/openoffice.org-2.4.0/program/soffice.bin soffice.bin.core
>
> and then run the command:
>
> "bt" and send me the output.
>
> I will have a look at your ktraces anyway.
>
> What kind of mozilla browser are you using?
>
> As per your root and non-root problem, can you check this:
>
> https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=88822
>
> and access rights and file ownership to your Mozilla profile?
>
> ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> !DSPAM:1660,480116b5548781643716331!
